commit.message:
feat: drawBondLines met lerpColor en toroidal-skip (closes #25)

Verbeterde implementatie van drawBondLines:
- lerpColor(ca, cb, 0.5) vervangt handmatig RGB-gemiddelde — idiomatischer p5.js
- strokeWeight(1.5) voor iets dikkere, beter zichtbare bindingslijnen
- alpha 110 (was 80) voor betere zichtbaarheid zonder te dominant te zijn
- Toroidal skip: bonds waarbij het kortste pad de canvasgrens kruist worden
  overgeslagen om canvas-brede diagonale lijnen te voorkomen

commit.message:
feat: add Vortaar rotation visualisation (closes #6.2)

Vortaar particles (type 1) now carry a rotAngle field initialised to 0.
Each frame the angle advances 0.04 rad, creating a smooth ~157-frame
full rotation. drawParticle() renders 3 small orbiting dots at 120 deg
intervals around the core (orbit radius 10 px) using the particle's own
rotAngle, giving each Vortaar a distinct spinning identity consistent
with its 'rotates around its own axis' trait.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add pgeocode and httpx to requirements.txt for Azure deploy

pgeocode used by territory_map router, httpx used by market_pulse router.
Both were missing from production dependencies causing import failure on Azure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(storage): encrypted storage — SQLCipher DB + unified Argon2id/HKDF key derivation

- Switch rusqlite to bundled-sqlcipher (AES-256 page-level encryption)
- Add HKDF domain separation: session key (construct-session-v1) and
  database key (construct-database-v1) derived from shared Argon2id master
- DerivedKeys + SessionKey structs hold both keys; master_salt generated
  once at account creation and stored in session.enc
- Storage::open(db_key) sets SQLCipher PRAGMA key before any other operation
- DB file created with 0o600 permissions
- Session file renamed session.json → session.enc with auto-migration
- app.rs: replace session_passphrase with session_key + current_session;
  defer start_orchestrator for new registrations until passphrase is set
- Fix all clippy -D warnings: remove use hex;, box large AuthMsg variant,
  add #[allow(dead_code)] to WIP scaffolding, collapse imports, fix
  too_many_arguments, remove unused imports across 8 files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Align SPC with MSBuild (#31)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement draw() loop with motion-blur, force, update, wrap and render steps (tasks 5.3 + 5.4)

Implements the main simulation draw loop in the correct order:
1. background(0,0,0,25) — semi-transparent fade produces motion-blur trail
2. applyForces() — O(n²) toroidal force calculation, returns bond pairs
3. tickFluxar() per particle — advances chaos clock (flips chaosSign every 120 frames)
4. updateParticles() — integrates acceleration into velocity with DAMPING, clips to maxSpeed, moves positions
5. wrapPosition() per particle — toroidal boundary wrapping
6. drawBondLines() then drawParticle() — bond lines drawn first so particles render on top

drawParticle and drawBondLines are added as minimal stubs; task 6.1 adds outer-glow,
task 6.2 adds Vortaar rotation, and task 6.3 enhances bond-line styling.
Tasks 5.3 and 5.4 are implemented together since 5.4 specifies exactly how the
fade-background step of 5.3 works — they are inseparable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add king travel system — royal progress and diplomatic visits

Kings can now embark on two types of trips:
- Royal Progress: tour own kingdom towns (up to 3), boosting happiness
  and prosperity in each visited town. Prioritizes troubled/large towns.
- Diplomatic Visit: travel to a foreign capital to improve relations.
  Weighted toward allies and friendly kingdoms. Blocked during war.

Travel decisions are personality+mood driven:
- Ambitious/brave/kind kings travel more often
- Paranoid/fearful/grieving kings never leave the capital
- Cowardly/lazy/content kings rarely travel
- Wrathful kings mostly stay home

Safety rules ensure king is home when needed:
- Returns immediately when war is declared (diplomatic trips)
- Returns for royal tournaments
- Won't travel during succession crisis or low treasury (<2000g)
- 60-day cooldown between trips (king is home ~75%+ of the time)
- Daily gold cost for travel (5g/day progress, 10g/day diplomatic)

Config: CONFIG.KING_TRAVEL with all tunable parameters.
UI: Travel status shown in kingdom detail panel and world overview.
Help: New 'King Travel' entry in help system.
Save compat: kingTravel and _lastTravelEndDay added to load defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix --update: track source clone path for git pull + re-copy

Installer now saves the git clone path to .source-path in the module
directory. --update reads this to git pull in the original clone, then
re-copies all module files and reinstalls the Python MCP package.

Previously --update checked for .git in the module dir (always a copy,
never a repo) so it always failed with 'not a git-based install'.

applyForces() already returned bonds[] with pairs where |F| > BOND_THRESHOLD.

- return value is always an Array

commit.message:
Add blog post: Why Multiple Models Matter - GitHub Copilot CLI's Subagent Architecture

This post explores how GitHub Copilot CLI enables parallel multi-model workflows through
subagents, allowing developers to mitigate blind spots by running multiple models (e.g.,
GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus) in parallel and consolidating their insights.

Key topics covered:
- Blind spot problem: every model has different weaknesses
- Spawning parallel reviews with /review command
- Workflow composition: /plan with live validation
- Rubber Duck experimental feature for automatic second opinions
- Cost efficiency: charged per user request, not per subagent
- Practical examples and getting started

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: Move k-means implementation from diskann-providers to diskann-disk (#933) K-means in `diskann-providers` was the last consumer of the old BLAS-based clustering path; PQ training has since migrated to `diskann-quantization`. The only active call site remaining was disk-index partitioning in `diskann-disk`. We will keep diskann-providers's implementation for now and move it to `diskann-disk`, rather than switching to the one in diskann-quantization, for the following reasons: - K-means in diskann-providers performs better at higher dimensions (>100): - K-means in diskann-providers supports multi-threading: We will work on closing these performance gaps and converging the two implementations in separate PRs. # Changes in this PR ## diskann-disk - Added `src/utils/kmeans.rs` — k-means implementation moved from `diskann-providers` - Added `src/utils/math_util.rs` — mathematical utilities (`compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_closest_centers`, `compute_closest_centers_in_block`, and helpers) extracted from `diskann-providers` and deduplicated - Exported `k_means_clustering`, `k_meanspp_selecting_pivots`, `run_lloyds`, `compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_closest_centers`, `compute_closest_centers_in_block` from `utils/mod.rs` - Updated `utils/partition.rs` to import kmeans functions and math utilities from local modules instead of `diskann-providers` - Moved kmeans criterion and iai-callgrind benchmarks from `diskann-providers/benches` to `diskann-disk/benches` - Added `proptest` and `approx` to dev-dependencies ## diskann-providers - Deleted `src/utils/kmeans.rs` - Removed `k_means_clustering`, `k_meanspp_selecting_pivots`, `run_lloyds`, `compute_vecs_l2sq`, `compute_vec_l2sq` from the public API - Removed the now-deduplicated math utility implementations from `math_util.rs` - Removed dead OPQ code: `generate_optimized_pq_pivots`, `opq_quantize_all_chunks`, `copy_chunk_centroids_to_full_table`, their test, and unused imports/constants — these were the sole remaining callers of k-means in this crate and were already gated behind `#[allow(dead_code)]` --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: arrayka <1551741+arrayka@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Alex Razumov (from Dev Box)

commit.message:
feat: clip particle velocity to maxSpeed after force application

Adds speed clipping in updateParticles() after DAMPING is applied.
Each particle's velocity magnitude is clamped to its type-specific
maxSpeed while preserving direction (unit-vector scaling).

Without this, fast particles (especially Fluxar maxSpeed=4.0 and
Lumion maxSpeed=3.5) could accelerate beyond their design limits,
breaking the emergent behaviour the interaction matrix is tuned for.

Also updates the 4.3 DAMPING unit tests to use sub-maxSpeed input
velocities so DAMPING and maxSpeed-clipping are tested independently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement updateParticles with DAMPING=0.98 velocity integration (task 4.3)

Adds updateParticles(particles) which per frame:
- integrates acceleration into velocity: v = (v + a) * DAMPING
- updates position: pos += v

DAMPING=0.98 ensures velocities decay gracefully each frame, preventing
particles from accelerating indefinitely and keeping the simulation stable.
Unit tests verify single-frame decay, two-frame decay, acceleration
integration, and position update correctness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix WT pane layout: use focus-pane instead of move-focus

Replace move-focus (keybinding action, silently fails as CLI subcommand)
with focus-pane -t <id> (documented CLI subcommand) for pane targeting.
Tracks pane IDs with monitor offset to correctly navigate the layout.

Fixes: all agents cramming into monitor's 20% column instead of
building a proper 2x2 grid in the remaining 80%.

commit.message:
Fix kings living in foreign kingdoms — relocate to own capital

installNewKing() never set kingdomId or townId, so succession could
crown someone who lived in another kingdom and leave them there.
Now installNewKing() sets kingdomId to the ruled kingdom and moves
the king to their capital (or any owned town).

Save-load fixup extended: checks kingdomId matches ruled kingdom,
verifies townId is in own territory, relocates to capital if not.

Per-tick enforcement in tickKingMood: if king drifts to foreign town,
immediately relocates them back to their capital.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: add MIN_DIST clamp unit tests for task 4.2

MIN_DIST=5 constant and clamp (max(d, MIN_DIST)) were already wired into
applyForces alongside task 4.1. This commit adds the dedicated unit tests
that verify the clamping behaviour:
- force at d=2 equals force at d=5 (both use dClamped=5, no singularity)
- force at d=10 is weaker than at d=5 (inverse-square law beyond MIN_DIST)

and proper occupation (king/reigning_queen). Also added per-tick enforcement
in tickKingMood to prevent drift during gameplay.

commit.message:
Convert mutable boolean flags to enums

Introduce 4 self-documenting enums to replace boolean flags:

- DeadEntryPolicy { Keep, Remove } — replaces keep_dead_entries: bool
  in bucket merge pipeline
- InitEntryPolicy { Preserve, NormalizeToLive } — replaces
  normalize_init_entries: bool in bucket merge pipeline
- KeyOrdering { LocalFirst, RemoteFirst } — replaces local_first: bool
  in crypto derive_shared_key()
- SortState { NeedsSorting, Presorted } — replaces presorted: bool
  in LedgerCloseData

Partial fix for #1136

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: chore(test): remove test artifact [skip ci]\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement applyForces O(n²) with MAX_RANGE early-exit (task 4.1)

- Define MAX_RANGE=150, MIN_DIST=5, DAMPING=0.98, BOND_THRESHOLD=0.5 constants
- applyForces(particles, fc, w, h) zeroes ax/ay, loops all pairs i<j
- Squared-distance early-exit before sqrt for performance
- Uses toroidalDelta for minimum-image distances (toroidal space)
- Unit vector uses d (not dClamped) to preserve direction accuracy
- dClamped=max(d,MIN_DIST) only in the 1/r² term to prevent singularity
- d===0 exact-overlap pairs are skipped (no NaN)
- Newton's 3rd law: opposite accelerations scaled by respective masses
- Returns bond pairs where |F| > BOND_THRESHOLD (for later rendering)
- 5 console.assert unit tests: early-exit, in-range, toroidal, Newton's 3rd, d=0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Matter (formerly Project CHIP) creates more connections between more objects, simplifying development for manufacturers and increasing compatibility for consumers, guided by the Connectivity Standards Alliance.

commit.message:
fix: Use Promise.allSettled for bulk delete to handle partial failures

- Changed from sequential await loop to parallel Promise.allSettled
- Allows multiple deletes to happen concurrently instead of one-by-one
- Better error handling for partial failures in bulk operations
- Shows user how many tenants failed to delete if any

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: make Rust coverage failures explicit

Print Rust line coverage, threshold, and the lowest-coverage files before failing the workflow, and upload the summary artifact.

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): fix agent pool validation query

az pipelines agent list requires --pool-id, not --pool-name.
Fix: resolve pool ID via 'az devops invoke' first, then list agents.
Also count only 'online' agents (filters out offline agents from prior runs).
